07 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • CENTCOM Commander Visits Aircraft Carrier in Arabian Sea CENTCOM 07 Feb 2026 -- The commander of U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) visited USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN 72), Feb. 7, as it transited the Arabian Sea during a scheduled deployment.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Turkish 66th Mechanized Infantry Brigade deploys to exercise STEADFAST DART NATO JFC Brunssum 07 Feb 2026 -- Türkiye's 66th Mechanized Infantry Brigade Command, part of NATO's Allied Reaction Force (ARF), arrived at Wunstdorf Air Base in northern Germany on Saturday, Feb. 7, 2026, to participate in exercise STEADFAST DART 2026.
  • Army corps commanders across nato gather to shape operations in 2026 NATO Allied Land Command 07 Feb 2026 -- Corps Commanders from across NATO's land forces met here January 29-30 to discuss plans for operationalizing their respective ability to execute NATO's integrated defense plans by aligning training, innovation, and investments.

  • Russia to Shape Policy on New START Treaty by Analyzing US Action Sputnik 07 Feb 2026 -- Russia will formulate its position on the New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty (New START) by analyzing US policy and the broader strategic landscape, Russia's permanent representative to the United Nations in Geneva, Gennady Gatilov, told Sputnik.

  • Military Assistance and Faster Delivery of Air Defense Systems and Aircraft: The President of Ukraine Met with the Minister of the Armed Forces of France President of Ukraine 07 Feb 2026

  • U.S. senators call on Legislature to pass Cabinet defense plan CNA 07 Feb 2026 -- Two U.S. senators on Friday urged Taiwan's political parties to swiftly pass a Cabinet-proposed NT$1.25 trillion (US$39.5 billion) special defense budget for 2026-2033, citing its importance to national security.
  • ANALYSIS / U.S. policy unchanged despite Taiwan omission in defense strategy: Analysts CNA 07 Feb 2026 -- The absence of Taiwan in the Pentagon's recently released 2026 National Defense Strategy (NDS) has raised concerns about the United States' commitment to the island nation's defense.
  • Defense ministry to buy time from U.S. to avoid derailing arms sales CNA 07 Feb 2026 -- The Ministry of National Defense (MND) said on Friday that it has received letters of offer and acceptance (LOA) for three systems covered by the Cabinet's NT$1.25 trillion (US$39.56 billion) special defense budget bill and will seek to extend the documents' term of validity to avoid stymieing the weapons sales.

  • Australia and Indonesia sign historic security treaty Prime Minister of Australia 07 Feb 2026

  • Royal New Zealand Navy's HMNZS Canterbury departs for mission to Tokelau New Zealand Defence Force 07 Feb 2026 -- Royal New Zealand Navy (RNZN) multi-role vessel HMNZS Canterbury will arrive into Samoa this weekend for a fuel stop as the ship begins its first South West Pacific maritime operation for 2026, Operation Calypso.
  • Showcasing missile with 'extraordinary destructive power' sign of Iran's refusal to renounce defense: IRGC Press TV 07 Feb 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC)'s deputy commander for political affairs has said the recent showcasing of a highly advanced ballistic missile with "extraordinary destructive power" reflects Iran's refusal to renounce its defensive might.

  • Indian Coast Guard Busts International Oil Smuggling Racket through Coordinated Sea-Air Operation India PIB 07 Feb 2026 -- The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) successfully busted an international oil smuggling racket through a meticulously planned sea-air coordinated operation on 05-06 February 2026

  • Netherlands approves plan to reduce military imports from Israeli-occupied territories Press TV 07 Feb 2026 -- The Netherlands House of Representatives has approved a plan to decrease the country's reliance on Israeli military and security imports, following heightened concern over Israel's human rights record and its involvement in serious violations of international law.

  • PH, US air forces conduct series of drills in Luzon Philippine News Agency 07 Feb 2026 -- Filipino and American aircraft conducted a series of exercises from Feb. 2 to 6 in various locations in Luzon as part of ongoing efforts to boost interoperability between the two services, the Philippine Air Force (PAF) announced on Saturday.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Few operational hospitals in Gaza struggle to provide care to those waiting: Health ministry Press TV 07 Feb 2026 -- Gaza's Health Ministry has warned that the few hospitals still operating in the war-ravaged territory are overwhelmed, leaving thousands of patients and wounded waiting for care as medical facilities struggle to provide services.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Naval and air forces of PLA Southern Theater Command conduct routine patrol in South China Sea PRC MND 07 Feb 2026 -- Senior Captain Zhai Shichen, spokesperson for the PLA Southern Theater Command stated, from February 2 to 6, the naval and air forces of the PLA Southern Theater Command conducted a routine patrol in the South China Sea.

  • Statement by Minister of International Development Åsmund Aukrust on the spread of famine in Sudan Government of Norway 07 Feb 2026
  • Saudi Arabia Condemns Rapid Support Forces Attacks on Civilians and Aid Convoys SPA 07 Feb 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's strong condemnation and denunciation of the criminal attacks carried out by the Rapid Support Forces against Al-Kuweik Military Hospital, a humanitarian aid convoy affiliated with the World Food Programme, and a vehicle transporting displaced civilians. These attacks resulted in the killing of tens of unarmed civilians, including women and children, and caused damage to humanitarian facilities and aid convoys in the states of North and South Kordofan

  • Guterres condemns escalating violence in South Sudan as aid operations come under fire UN News 07 Feb 2026 -- The UN Secretary-General has strongly condemned the surge in violence across South Sudan, warning that civilians and aid workers are paying a devastating price as humanitarian operations are increasingly targeted.
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on South Sudan UN Secretary-General 07 Feb 2026

News Reports

  • US intel. chief blocked report on 'call between foreign intelligence agent and Trump associate': Whistleblower Press TV 07 Feb 2026 -- A whistleblower says the United States national intelligence chief blocked routine processing of a report exposing a call between an individual linked to foreign intelligence and a person close to Donald Trump.

  • Suspects Reportedly Detained Over Shooting Of Top Russian Intelligence General RFE/RL 07 Feb 2026 -- Russian law enforcement agencies have detained a suspect and his alleged accomplice in an apparent assassination attempt on the deputy chief of the GRU military intelligence agency, Vladimir Alekseyev, Russian media reported on February 7.

  • UN chief strongly condemns attack on mosque in Pakistan People's Daily 07 Feb 2026 -- UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres strongly condemns Friday's attack on a mosque in the Pakistani capital of Islamabad, his deputy spokesperson said.

  • Statement of the Permanent Representative of the Russian Federation to the United Nations Office and Other International Organizations in Geneva Ambassador Gennady Gatilov at the plenary meeting of the Conference on Disarmament on the expiration of the New START Treaty, Geneva, February 6, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 Feb 2026

  • China's State Council discusses draft gov't work report, 15th Five-Year Plan People's Daily 07 Feb 2026 -- China's State Council on Friday convened a plenary meeting to discuss the draft government work report and a draft outline of the 15th Five-Year Plan for economic and social development.
  • Chinese embassy rejects Norway's 2026 threat assessment as "speculative China threat narrative," urges end to smears Global Times 07 Feb 2026 -- The Chinese Embassy in Norway on Saturday issued a statement expressing strong dissatisfaction and firm opposition to the annual threat assessment reports released by Norway's Police Security Service (PST), the Norwegian Intelligence Service (NIS) and the National Security Authority (NSM).
  • China successfully launches reusable experimental spacecraft Global Times 07 Feb 2026 -- China on Saturday successfully launched a reusable experimental spacecraft aboard a Long March-2F carrier rocket from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center, according to Xinhua News Agency.
  • China successfully launches reusable experimental spacecraft People's Daily 07 Feb 2026 -- China succeeded in launching a reusable experimental spacecraft via a Long March-2F carrier rocket from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center in northwest China on Saturday.

  • Cabinet to seek cross-party support for U.S. trade pact, MOU: Source CNA 07 Feb 2026 -- The Cabinet will seek cross-party support for the Agreement on Reciprocal Trade (ART) between Taiwan and the United States, as well as an accompanying memorandum of understanding (MOU) signed in January, a source told CNA Saturday.
  • Self-help Taiwan's best protection: European lawmakers CNA 07 Feb 2026 -- Taiwan must first strengthen its own national defense to deter a potential invasion by China as cross-strait tensions continue to rise, multiple European lawmakers told CNA on Friday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 07 Feb 2026

  • Iran will not start a war but will not hesitate to defend national security, top general says IRNA 07 Feb 2026 -- Chairman of the Chief of Staff of Iran's Armed Forces Major General Abdolrahim Mousavi says that Iran will never initiate war but will not hesitate to defend its national security, vital interests, and territorial integrity.
  • Iran will not negotiate over its missiles: Araghchi IRNA 07 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran will not negotiate over its missile program, describing it as a purely defensive matter.
  • PGCC welcomes Iran-US talks in Oman IRNA 07 Feb 2026 -- Secretary-General of the Persian Gulf Cooperation Council (PGCC) Jasem Mohamed AlBudaiwi has welcomed the new round of indirect negotiations between Iran and the United States in Oman.
  • Iran Says Nuclear Talks With US Should Resume Soon After Oman Meeting RFE/RL 07 Feb 2026 -- Iran's foreign minister said Tehran and Washington believe nuclear talks should resume soon following indirect negotiations in Oman, even as Tehran ruled out discussions on its missile program and uranium enrichment.
  • Araghchi says venue of next round of nuclear talks with US could change Press TV 07 Feb 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the venue of the next round of indirect talks with the United States could change.
  • Iran will target US military bases in region if attacked, Araghchi warns Press TV 07 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has warned that in the event of an open aggression by the United States, American military bases in West Asia will be targeted.
  • Iran Would Hit US Bases in Persian Gulf If Attacked - Foreign Minister Sputnik 07 Feb 2026 -- Iran would strike American bases in the Persian Gulf region if the United States attacks, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Saturday.
  • Any anti-Iran adventure will bring 'strategic defeat' to enemies, destabilize entire region: Top general Press TV 07 Feb 2026 -- Iran's top military official warns that any adventurist act against the Islamic Republic will bring "strategic defeat" to enemies and spread war across the entire region.
  • New Aggression Will Trigger 'Absolute and Strategic Defeat' of Iran's Enemies: Top General Sputnik 07 Feb 2026 -- "The enemies of the Islamic Republic of Iran are well aware that any adventure or attempt to impose war against Islamic Iran will not only lead to their absolute and strategic defeat but will also cause the expansion of war and crisis throughout the region," Armed Forces Chief of Staff Abdolrahim Mousavi warned.
  • IRGC forces capture nearly a dozen senior PJAK members in western Iran Press TV 07 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Ground Force has captured nearly a dozen senior members of the so-called Party for Free Life in Kurdistan (PJAK) militant group in the western province of Kermanshah on the border with Iraq.
  • Talks with U.S. "a good start;" both sides agree to continue negotiations -- Iranian FM People's Daily 07 Feb 2026 -- The indirect talks between Iran and the United States in Oman's Muscat were "a good start," with both sides agreeing to continue negotiations in the future, Iran's Fars News Agency reported Friday, citing Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi.
  • Trump says more talks on Iran next week People's Daily 07 Feb 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Friday that his administration will hold more talks on Iran early next week, hours after having a "very good" indirect dialogue with Iran in Oman amid high tensions.
  • Trump administration imposes new sanctions on targets linked to Iran's oil trade People's Daily 07 Feb 2026 -- The Trump administration on Friday announced new sanctions targeting 15 entities, two individuals and 14 so-called shadow fleet vessels linked to Iran's oil trade.
  • Trump signs tariff threat against Iran's trade partners shortly after high-stakes talks in Oman Press TV 07 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has signed an executive order threatening additional tariffs on countries that continue to trade with Iran, shortly after Iranian and American representatives held indirect talks in Muscat on Tehran's nuclear energy program and sanctions relief.

  • US to lift Russia-related tariffs on India following preliminary trade deal Press TV 07 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has signed an executive order removing a 25-percent tariff on Indian goods that had been imposed over India's purchases of Russian oil, following a preliminary trade agreement reached earlier this week between the United States and India.

  • 6th Round of Bilateral Political Consultations between Pakistan and Canada Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 Feb 2026 -- The 6th Round of Bilateral Political Consultations between Pakistan and Canada was held today in Islamabad. On behalf of Pakistan, Ambassador Nabeel Munir, Special Secretary (United Nations) joined by Director General (Americas) participated in the Consultations while the Canadian side was represented by Ambassador Weldon Epp, Assistant Deputy Minister (Indo-Pacific) from Global Affairs Canada and the High Commissioner Tarik Ali Khan in Islamabad, Pakistan.
  • Pakistan Space & Upper Atmosphere Research Commission (SUPARCO) has achieved yet another significant milestone in Pakistan's Human Spaceflight Programme with the successful completion of the secondary phase of astronaut candidates' selection. ISPR Pakistan 07 Feb 2026
  • UNICEF statement on attack in Islamabad, Pakistan UNICEF 07 Feb 2026 -- Statement by UNICEF Representative in Pakistan, Pernille Ironside

  • Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Radosław Sikorski visits United States Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 Feb 2026 -- Poland's top diplomat Radosław Sikorski visited the United States between 4 and 7 February 2026, where he took part in the Critical Minerals Ministerial.
